title: senna symonii
intro: Senna symonii, also known as Tanner's cassia, is a shrub native to tropical Africa. It is a member of the legume family and is used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative due to its high concentration of anthraquinones. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including constipation, diarrhea, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Senna symonii can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
